story: Cheers erupted as the puck dropped in a brand-new ice palace. The Montreal Forum, a legendary hockey arena, opened its doors in 1924. This building would become the heart of hockey in Canada, hosting countless thrilling games and creating lifelong memories for generations of fans.
activity_question: What opened in 1924?
follow_up_question: What sport was the Montreal Forum famous for?

title: Puppets Take Over TV!
story: Imagine a world without TV puppets! That changed in 1948 when 'Kukla, Fran, & Ollie' burst onto screens. This fun show, starring Fran Allison and her puppet pals, made its big debut on November 29. Kids and grown-ups alike fell in love with the silly characters and their adventures on NBC's Chicago station.
activity_question: Who starred in the puppet TV show 'Kukla, Fran, & Ollie'?
follow_up_question: What kind of characters were Kukla and Ollie?

title: Srinath's Speedy Start in Test Cricket
story: Javagal Srinath thundered onto the cricket scene in 1991. The fast bowler from India made his Test debut on November 29, facing off against Australia at the Gabba. Known for his lightning-quick deliveries, Srinath's arrival marked the beginning of a new era for Indian pace bowling.
activity_question: Where did Javagal Srinath make his Test cricket debut?
bonus_fact: The Gabba is a nickname for the Brisbane Cricket Ground in Australia.

title: Hornet Takes Flight: A Mighty Carrier
story: Waves crashed against the hull as a massive ship slid into the water. The USS Hornet, a powerful aircraft carrier, joined the US Navy on November 29, 1943. This floating fortress could launch planes to defend America during World War II. Sailors cheered as their new home set sail, ready to carry aircraft into battle across vast oceans.
activity_question: What type of ship was the USS Hornet?
bonus_fact: Aircraft carriers are like floating airports, with a long flat deck for planes to take off and land.

title: Frosty Record: Netherlands Shivers
story: Brrr! Thermometers in the Netherlands plunged to an icy -14.0°C on November 29, 1921. This bone-chilling temperature set the record for the coldest November day ever recorded in the country. People bundled up in extra layers, and hot cocoa became a must-have drink to stay warm during this frosty spell.
activity_question: What was the record-breaking temperature on this cold day?
follow_up_question: How do you think people stayed warm during such extreme cold?

title: Dancing Queen: Pearl Primus Takes the Stage
story: Pearl Primus leaped onto the world stage in 1919, born in Port of Spain, Trinidad and Tobago on November 29. This dynamic dancer would go on to become a trailblazer, introducing African dance to American audiences. Primus's powerful performances and choreography celebrated the beauty of African culture, inspiring generations of dancers to follow in her graceful footsteps.
activity_question: Where was Pearl Primus born?
bonus_fact: Pearl Primus was not just a dancer, but also an anthropologist who studied African dance traditions.

title: Stargazer Extraordinaire: Benjamin Markarian
story: Galaxies twinkled with possibility when Benjamin Markarian entered the world in 1913. Born on November 29 in Shaumiani, Russian Empire, this future Armenian astrophysicist would revolutionize our understanding of the cosmos. Markarian became famous for identifying a special group of galaxies with bright blue centers, now known as Markarian galaxies. His work opened new windows into the mysteries of space.
activity_question: What did Benjamin Markarian become famous for identifying?
activity_choices: A special group of galaxies with bright blue centers
A comet in our solar system
activity_answer: A special group of galaxies with bright blue centers
follow_up_question: Can you imagine what it would be like to discover something new in space?

title: The Spark That Lit Up Radio
story: Zap! A baby boy born in Lancaster, England in 1849 would grow up to electrify the world. John Ambrose Fleming arrived on November 29, destined to become a brilliant inventor. His creation of the vacuum diode, the first electronic rectifier of radio waves, revolutionized wireless communication. Fleming's spark of genius helped pave the way for the radio age.
activity_question: What did John Ambrose Fleming invent?
follow_up_question: How did Fleming's invention help change communication?
